name: Anna Petrova
message: We receive 40-60 candidate applications per week and manually read every CV. We need a simple workflow that summarizes each candidate, scores fit for a role, and flags risks for a recruiter.
company_summary: Recruiting studio handling candidate applications for client roles.
likely_pain_points: Manual CV review; slow candidate shortlisting; inconsistent risk notes.
automation_opportunity: Candidate screening workflow: form/table intake, AI summary, fit score, risks, next step for recruiter.
ai_summary: Needs an AI workflow to summarize candidates, score fit, and flag recruiter risks.
ai_reason: Urgent timeline and clear operational pain, but small budget.
next_step: Offer a low-cost prototype using synthetic candidate data and Google Sheets.
personalized_reply: Hi Anna, I can show a simple candidate screening demo: each application gets a summary, fit score, risk flags, and a suggested recruiter action in one table.

name: Mark Evans
message: Our ecommerce support team spends too much time sorting product questions, refund requests, and delivery issues. We want AI to classify incoming requests and draft the next response.
company_summary: Ecommerce/home goods company with recurring support requests.
likely_pain_points: Support overload; manual sorting; repeated refund/delivery/product questions.
automation_opportunity: Support triage workflow: classify request type, priority, suggested reply, and status in Sheets/CRM.
ai_summary: Wants AI to classify ecommerce support messages and draft next responses.
ai_reason: Good budget and strong use case, but timeline is moderate.
next_step: Ask for sample request categories and propose a 7-day support triage demo.
personalized_reply: Hi Mark, your support flow is a strong fit for AI triage. I can prototype a table where each request is categorized, prioritized, and paired with a draft response.

name: Sofia Klein
message: Patients submit appointment and billing questions through our website. We need help routing requests, summarizing messages, and sending urgent cases to a human manager.
company_summary: Local clinic receiving patient appointment and billing requests.
likely_pain_points: Manual routing; urgent cases mixed with routine questions; healthcare context requires human review.
automation_opportunity: Patient request triage: classify appointment/billing/urgent, summarize, and notify a human manager.
ai_summary: Needs request routing and summaries for clinic website inquiries.
ai_reason: High budget and urgent timeline; sensitive domain means human review is required.
next_step: Propose a conservative prototype with synthetic data and clear human approval step.
personalized_reply: Hi Sofia, I would keep this human-in-the-loop: AI can classify and summarize incoming requests, while urgent or sensitive cases are always routed to a manager.

name: Daniel Brooks
message: We manage leads for several small clients and lose time copying form submissions into sheets. We want to understand what could be automated with AI before we buy anything.
company_summary: Marketing agency managing leads for several small clients.
likely_pain_points: Copy-paste between forms and sheets; unclear automation scope; low buying urgency.
automation_opportunity: Mini-audit: map lead intake, remove manual copying, add lead summary and priority fields.
ai_summary: Wants consultation on what can be automated before buying.
ai_reason: Clear pain but low urgency and low budget.
next_step: Offer a free/cheap mini-audit and use it as discovery rather than a full build.
personalized_reply: Hi Daniel, a short mini-audit would make sense first: I can map where leads are copied manually and show which parts can be automated without buying a full system.

name: Mira Hassan
message: Students send many questions about courses, payments, and schedules. We want a workflow that categorizes requests, summarizes intent, and suggests the next action for support.
company_summary: Online school with student support and course/payment/schedule questions.
likely_pain_points: High volume repetitive support; slow response; manual category handling.
automation_opportunity: Student support triage: classify question type, summarize intent, suggest response/action, flag urgent payment issues.
ai_summary: Needs a fast AI workflow for student support categorization and next actions.
ai_reason: Immediate timeline, clear repetitive process, and realistic MVP scope.
next_step: Propose a 7-day demo using 20 synthetic student messages and support categories.
personalized_reply: Hi Mira, this is a good 7-day AI support workflow: each student message can be categorized, summarized, and assigned a next action for your team.

name: Owen Clarke
message: We get service requests from calls and website forms. Managers need to know which requests are urgent, what the client needs, and which technician should follow up first.
company_summary: Local plumbing/service company handling incoming repair requests.
likely_pain_points: Urgency triage; unclear client needs; technician assignment delays.
automation_opportunity: Service request classifier: urgency, job type, location/need summary, suggested technician/team follow-up.
ai_summary: Needs AI to prioritize service requests and clarify next technician action.
ai_reason: Urgent, practical, and easy to demonstrate with form-to-sheet workflow.
next_step: Ask for common request types and build a simple urgent/non-urgent routing prototype.
personalized_reply: Hi Owen, I can build a small demo where each request gets urgency, job type, short summary, and the recommended follow-up action for the manager.

name: Elena Rossi
message: Our recruiters manually compare candidate profiles with job requirements. We need a demo system for candidate summary, fit score, risks, and next step.
company_summary: HR partners/recruiting company comparing candidates to roles.
likely_pain_points: Manual candidate-role matching; inconsistent evaluation; slow recruiter notes.
automation_opportunity: AI HR screening demo: candidate summary, fit score, risks, next step, human review status.
ai_summary: Needs a structured HR screening demo for candidate evaluation.
ai_reason: Strong budget and exact fit to second portfolio case; moderate urgency.
next_step: Offer HR screening as second demo case after lead classifier.
personalized_reply: Hi Elena, I can prototype an HR screening table that creates candidate summaries, fit scores, risk notes, and suggested recruiter next steps from test profiles.

name: Victor Chen
message: Customer messages arrive from different forms and messengers. We need a way to collect requests in one sheet, classify them, and notify operations about urgent delivery problems.
company_summary: Delivery company with customer requests across multiple channels.
likely_pain_points: Fragmented intake; urgent delivery issues missed; operations notifications delayed.
automation_opportunity: Multi-channel request intake: normalize into one sheet, classify urgency/type, notify operations for critical delivery issues.
ai_summary: Needs centralized intake, classification, and urgent operations notifications.
ai_reason: High budget, urgent timeline, and strong operational value.
next_step: Propose a staged pilot: first Google Form/Sheet demo, then channel integrations.
personalized_reply: Hi Victor, I would start with one controlled intake table, classify each request by type and urgency, then notify operations only when delivery risk is high.

name: Laura Smith
message: We want to test whether AI can summarize discovery calls and client briefs. At this stage we only need a low-cost prototype and clear recommendation.
company_summary: Design studio working with discovery calls and client briefs.
likely_pain_points: Manual call/brief summaries; inconsistent requirements capture; low-cost prototype need.
automation_opportunity: Brief summarizer: paste transcript/brief, extract project goals, requirements, risks, and next questions.
ai_summary: Wants a lightweight AI prototype for summarizing client discovery materials.
ai_reason: Low budget but clear MVP and useful portfolio case.
next_step: Offer a manual/semi-automated prototype using sample briefs before API automation.
personalized_reply: Hi Laura, a low-cost prototype is realistic: we can use sample briefs to generate summaries, risks, missing info, and next questions for the design team.

name: Nikolai Morozov
message: Our dispatch team receives requests for quotes by email and web form. We need AI to extract route, cargo type, urgency, and suggested next step for the sales team.
company_summary: Logistics company processing quote requests for dispatch and sales.
likely_pain_points: Manual extraction of route/cargo/urgency; slow sales follow-up; quote data scattered.
automation_opportunity: RFQ parser: extract route, cargo type, urgency, missing fields, and next sales step into a structured sheet.
ai_summary: Needs AI extraction from quote requests for dispatch and sales follow-up.
ai_reason: Immediate urgency, strong budget, and very structured extraction task.
next_step: Propose a quote request parser demo with 10 synthetic RFQs and sales next steps.
personalized_reply: Hi Nikolai, this is a strong fit for structured extraction: AI can pull route, cargo, urgency, missing data, and the next sales action into one table.
